args.title: New patient intake – Emma Lee (SLP referral)

args.body:
Dear Daniel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy! We received the referral from Dr. Priya Nair for Emma and are glad you're connecting with us.

We have noted your preference for after-school appointments on Tuesdays or Thursdays, and our team is working to confirm an evaluation slot that fits your schedule. A staff member will be in touch shortly to confirm the appointment, go over any insurance details, and share new-patient paperwork.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to call our front desk.

We look forward to welcoming Emma!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

Draft acknowledgement email (draft_01ksraacz6jg9f0qkd20b478f9) is ready for review and sending.

Message contains possible safeguarding disclosure: "his dad started getting rough with him during weekends." Child is showing behavioral changes (clinginess). Leo is NOT a registered patient. Clinical lead must assess whether mandated-reporter obligations are triggered and direct next steps BEFORE any scheduling or outbound contact occurs. Do NOT ask the family investigative questions. Draft outbound message is ready for clinical lead approval before release.
args.title: SAME-HOUR P0 Safeguarding Review — Leo Gomez (age 6)

args.body: Hi Maria, thank you so much for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy about Leo. We received your voicemail and want you to know that we take every message seriously. A member of our team will be in touch with you shortly. If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to call us. We look forward to speaking with you soon.

items.recommended_next_action: Same-hour clinical lead review is required before ANY outbound contact or scheduling. Escalation esc_01ksrabqnhs263dgrs7mcrfgc0 (P0) and task task_01ksrabqnhh804sfc97qtrkwgx have been created and assigned to the clinical lead. Clinical lead must assess mandated-reporter obligations and approve or modify draft_01ksrabqnja14m025gdvy9k5sq before it is released to the family. No scheduling, no slot holds, and no investigative questions to the family until clinical lead directs otherwise.
items.draft_reply: Hi Maria, thank you so much for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy about Leo. We received your voicemail and want you to know that we take every message seriously. escalation.reason: Voicemail from parent (Maria Gomez) includes disclosure that child Leo (age 6) has been experiencing a parent "getting rough with him." This language constitutes a potential safeguarding/abuse disclosure requiring immediate mandated-reporter review by the clinical lead, per Cedar Kids safeguarding policy. Child is not yet a registered patient.

items.decision_rationale: The voicemail from Maria Gomez contains an embedded safeguarding disclosure: she states that Leo's father has been 'getting rough with him' during weekends, and that Leo has become more clingy — a behavioral change consistent with a trauma response. This language constitutes a potential physical abuse disclosure triggering Cedar Kids' mandated-reporter obligations regardless of the surface-level scheduling request. Per safeguarding policy and urgency calibration rules, the safeguarding signal overrides the routine scheduling wrapper, requiring P0 classification and same-hour clinical lead review. Leo is not a registered patient (confirmed via search_patient). No insurance information was provided, so verification was not possible. A neutral, non-investigative draft reply has been prepared for clinical lead approval but has NOT been sent. No slots have been found or held. All next steps are gated on clinical lead direction.

args.title: New OT referral – Owen Brooks – pending insurance clearance

args.body:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy. We have received the referral from Dr. Helena Yu at Westside Pediatrics for Owen's occupational therapy evaluation, and we're glad you've connected with us.

Before we proceed with scheduling, a member of our team will be in touch with you shortly to go over your insurance benefits and discuss any coverage details for our services. We want to make sure you have all the information you need before moving forward.

Once that conversation is complete, we'll work with you to find a morning appointment time that fits your schedule.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to call us.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Parent asking whether inability to produce R sounds at age 4 is developmentally typical

args.body:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you so much for reaching out about Ava — it's clear how much you care about her development, and questions like this are completely understandable!

While we'd love to give you a straightforward answer, our front-desk team isn't able to offer clinical guidance on whether a specific speech pattern is typical or a cause for concern — that determination really does need the trained eye (and ear!) of a licensed speech-language pathologist.

The good news is that we offer **speech-language evaluations** here at Cedar Kids Therapy specifically for situations like yours. An SLP evaluation is the best way to get a personalized, professional answer to your question — whether that turns out to be "perfectly on track, no worries!" or "here's how we can help."

If you'd like to take that next step, we'd be happy to walk you through what an evaluation involves, go over scheduling options, and answer any questions about insurance or cost before you commit to anything.

Feel free to reply here or call our office and a member of our team will be glad to help!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

args.notes: Parent Jordan Kim sent a portal message asking whether it's developmentally normal for their 4-year-old, Ava Kim, to not yet produce R sounds. This is a clinical question we cannot answer via message per policy. Draft reply (draft_01ksrafpx1gmqrt0z1wjwn7maq) has been prepared — it acknowledges the concern warmly, explains we can't advise clinically, and invites Jordan to book an SLP evaluation. No existing patient record found for Ava Kim. No insurance or DOB provided yet. If Jordan expresses interest in booking, intake should gather DOB, insurance/payer info, and preferred scheduling times before finding and holding a slot.
args.title: Review & send reply to Jordan Kim – clinical question re: Ava's R sounds

Intake task task_01ksrafxncgtkyq59fx1jsr1kf is queued — if Jordan expresses interest in booking, gather DOB, payer, and availability before finding and holding a slot.

items.decision_rationale: This is a routine clinical question from the parent of a prospective patient (no existing record found for Ava Kim). Per clinical_advice policy, front-desk and automated systems must not opine on whether a developmental pattern is typical or concerning — that requires a licensed SLP evaluation. There are no safeguarding signals and no same-day operational urgency, so P2 is appropriate. The drafted reply is empathetic, non-clinical, and routes Jordan toward an SLP evaluation without committing to scheduling. Insurance cannot be verified and a slot cannot be held until the family expresses intent to book and provides the required intake information.

args.title: Incomplete referral – Sam Taylor (SLP) – missing DOB, guardian, insurance

args.body:
Dear Lakeview Pediatrics team,

Thank you for sending the referral for Sam Taylor to Cedar Kids Therapy. We're glad to work with you on this child's care.

Unfortunately, we are unable to begin intake processing because several required fields are missing from the referral. Could you please re-submit or call us with the following information?

1. Child's date of birth
2. Parent/guardian name and contact information (phone and/or email)
3. Insurance payer name
4. Insurance member ID

Once we have these details, we will promptly verify coverage and reach out to the family to schedule an SLP evaluation.

Please re-fax the completed referral or call our intake line at your earliest convenience. We look forward to supporting Sam.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy – Intake Team
args.recipient: Lakeview Pediatrics / Dr. Omar Keene office

args.title: New patient intake – Isabella Lopez (SLP eval, new referral)

args.body:
Hola Ana, le habla el equipo de Cedar Kids Therapy. Muchas gracias por comunicarse con nosotros para su hija Isabella.

Recibimos su mensaje y nos alegra informarle que aceptamos Medicaid. Hemos encontrado disponibilidad con Lucía Morales, nuestra terapeuta de habla bilingüe (español e inglés), el jueves 30 de abril a las 11:00 a.m. Estamos reservando provisionalmente ese horario para Isabella mientras un miembro de nuestro equipo confirma los detalles con usted.

En breve, alguien de nuestro equipo le llamará al número 555-0107 para confirmar la cita, recopilar la información de registro de Isabella y enviarle los documentos de admisión en español.

Si tiene alguna pregunta, no dude en comunicarse con nosotros. ¡Esperamos conocer a Isabella pronto!
